mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but it may require careful resource management and tuning to maximize performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface to interact with the llm-d platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to point to the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that all nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d application using Helm, which simplifies deployment and management.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Adds the llm-d Helm repository.
> Installs llm-d in its own namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstalls the llm-d application.
> Deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step upgrades the llm-d deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp llm-d-pod:/path/to/data /local/backup

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup your data from the llm-d pod.
> Upgrades the llm-d application.
> Verify that the llm-d pods are running and ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image does not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource requests/limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or adjust resource requests in the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The request to the Kubernetes API server is timing out.

**Fix:**
Check the network connectivity and ensure the API server is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
